talk_metadata.talk_title: CID-078, a first‑in‑class oral macrocycle Cyclin AB RXL inhibitor
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we often get asked how you're able to obtain selectivity over the other cyclins, given the hydrophobic patch is so highly conserved"
Speaker noted: "we have a Phase I trial ongoing now"

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Large macrocycle (~1000 Da) oral cyclin AB inhibitor
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted the high molecular weight and need for extensive SAR to balance permeability and oral exposure, suggesting limited chemical space for further optimization
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad enrollment without mandatory RB/ E2F biomarker stratification may dilute signal

key_data_gaps: No human Phase I efficacy or safety data presented
No OS or PFS endpoints reported

crispro_opportunity.description: RB loss and E2F‑high status are proposed biomarkers but were not incorporated into a prospective trial design; CrisPRO could develop a predictive model to prospectively select patients
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "most patients will come on our trial, they'll be scored as having an RB alteration... we need to understand the type of alteration"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's computational pipeline to integrate genomic (RB1 mutation) and proteomic (RB protein loss) data to generate a composite RB/E2F activity score for patient selection

crispro_opportunity.description: Preclinical data suggest synergy with CDK4/6 inhibitor‑resistant tumors; CrisPRO could model combination schedules with existing CDK4/6 inhibitors
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Audience question: "It should work in CD4/6 inhibitor‑resistant model, right?"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate pharmacodynamic interactions between CID‑078 and CDK4/6 inhibitors to prioritize dosing regimens

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal RB/E2F biomarker assay to prospectively enroll patients?
Will CID‑078 retain activity in tumors with alternative mechanisms of E2F activation beyond RB loss?

talk_metadata.talk_title: AMX883, an oral BRD9 degrader for AML
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we saw no emergence of resistance to venetoclax when cultured with venetoclax and AMX883"
Speaker noted: "we anticipate this being in the clinic in the second half of this year"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential cardiac QT liability observed with earlier BRD9 PROTACs, not fully addressed for AMX883
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker: "we cannot see any QT liability for this compound pre‑clinically"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Oral degradation of BRD9 to relieve differentiation block in AML
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Broad AML enrollment without mandatory BRD9 expression or non‑canonical BAF dependency biomarker
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: BRD9 protein level or non‑canonical BAF activity assay
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Early‑phase single‑arm design may lack comparator to assess true efficacy

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Covalent reversible cyanoacrylamide warhead targeting DCAF16
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker admitted rapid decomplexation and difficulty detecting ternary complex, suggesting a narrow window for covalent engagement
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: High mouse doses (100 mg/kg) required to achieve nanomolar exposure indicate suboptimal PK in rodents

key_data_gaps: No human safety or efficacy data presented
Lack of comparative arm to standard AML therapy

crispro_opportunity.description: BRD9 dependency is inferred from AML subtype but not prospectively validated; CrisPRO could develop a transcriptomic signature of non‑canonical BAF reliance
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker: "BRD9 expression is elevated in AML, and SMARCB1 is downregulated"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply machine‑learning to AML RNA‑seq datasets to predict BRD9 dependency and enrich trial enrollment

crispro_opportunity.description: Synergy observed with venetoclax; CrisPRO can model optimal dosing schedules for BRD9 degrader + BCL2 inhibition
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate pharmacodynamic interaction to prioritize combination cohorts

cited_competitors.name: BRD4 PROTAC programs
cited_competitors.context: Questioned why BRD9 rather than BRD4 was targeted

unresolved_questions: What is the therapeutic window for BRD9 degradation in normal hematopoietic cells?
Will AMX883 overcome venetoclax resistance in patients with high MCL1 expression?

talk_metadata.talk_title: JNJ‑2175, an ENPP3‑targeted ADC for solid tumors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see broad and high ENPP3 expression across several solid tumors"
Speaker noted: "the molecule is in Phase 1 dose‑escalation study"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential heme‑related toxicity from basophil activation not experimentally addressed
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker could not comment on Fc‑silent design mitigating heme risk, indicating uncertainty

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: ADC delivering auristatin payload to ENPP3‑positive tumor cells
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all ENPP3‑positive solid tumors without stratifying by tumor penetration or payload sensitivity
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Quantitative ENPP3 expression threshold linked to in‑vivo efficacy
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: No comparator arm; early‑phase single‑arm may mask modest activity

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker indicated reliance on licensed platform; core payload chemistry not proprietary
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Use of a single payload limits flexibility if resistance to microtubule agents emerges

key_data_gaps: No human efficacy or safety data presented
Lack of detailed PK/PD correlation in patients

crispro_opportunity.description: ENPP3 IHC positivity alone may not predict ADC delivery; CrisPRO could integrate tumor microenvironment features to refine patient selection
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker: "we see broad ENPP3 expression, but also noted variable tumor regression timing"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Combine IHC with spatial transcriptomics to model ADC penetration and payload release

crispro_opportunity.description: Previous ENPP3 ADC attempts suffered heme toxicity; CrisPRO could redesign linker‑payload to reduce basophil activation
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Audience: "ENPP3 ADCs have been plagued by heme tox"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use computational chemistry to screen alternative non‑auristatin payloads with lower basophil activation potential

cited_competitors.name: Previous ENPP3 ADC programs
cited_competitors.context: Question about heme toxicity observed in earlier attempts

unresolved_questions: What is the minimal ENPP3 density required for effective ADC internalization?
Does Fc‑silencing materially reduce basophil‑mediated toxicity?

talk_metadata.talk_title: IPN‑1203, a dual Vβ6/Vβ10 T‑cell agonist with IL‑15 co‑stimulation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we see a marked reduction in exhausted cell population, more than 50%"
Speaker noted: "we are now in phase one"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Patients lacking sufficient Vβ6/Vβ10 TCR repertoire may not respond, but no pre‑screening strategy discussed
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker emphasized expansion from ~6% baseline to ~60% in vitro, implying a subset of patients may have low baseline frequencies

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Selective activation of Vβ6/Vβ10 T cells with IL‑15 co‑stimulation
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Broad solid‑tumor enrollment without baseline Vβ6/Vβ10 TCR frequency assessment
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Single‑arm dose escalation may not detect lack of efficacy in low‑repertoire patients

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Selectivity relies on a single high‑affinity Vβ epitope; other companies may develop broader Vβ panels that could outcompete
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: No NK cell expansion observed, potentially limiting activity in tumors where NK cells are critical

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed scRNA‑seq data indicating strong central‑memory imprinting, yet concluded that "minimal cytokine release" will ensure safety, ignoring the potential for delayed cytokine storm from expanded memory T cells

key_data_gaps: No human pharmacodynamic data on Vβ6/Vβ10 expansion
Absence of comparative arm to standard immunotherapy

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a CrisPRO model to predict patient Vβ6/Vβ10 repertoire size from peripheral blood sequencing and select responders
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker: "binding in PBMCs reflects prevalence of Vβ6/Vβ10 around 6%"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage CrisPRO's TCR repertoire analytics to generate a cutoff for enrollment

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine IPN‑1203 with checkpoint inhibitors to further relieve exhaustion; CrisPRO can simulate synergistic effects
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted reduction of exhausted T cells as a key benefit
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model PD‑L1 blockade plus Vβ‑selective activation to prioritize combination cohorts

cited_competitors.name: AstraZeneca non‑selective Vβ TCE
cited_competitors.context: Questioned why IPN‑1203 chose selective Vβ6/Vβ10 versus non‑selective approach

unresolved_questions: What is the minimal baseline Vβ6/Vβ10 frequency required for clinical efficacy?
Will IL‑15 co‑stimulation cause off‑target NK cell activation in patients?
